content: Integration tests spawning subprocesses in Node.js CI: Common flakiness causes: (1) Temp dir cleanup races — if a test fails mid-execution and cleanup() throws, subsequent tests may inherit stale state; process.on('exit') backstop pattern in helpers.mjs is correct but does not fire on SIGKILL; (2) maxBuffer exhaustion — execFileSync default is 1MB, agentmap on large repos (laravel/framework = 400k+ lines) can exceed this; helpers.mjs uses 64MB which is correct; (3) Parallel test file execution by node --test (default: os.availableParallelism()-1 child processes) means multiple tests may hit os.tmpdir() simultaneously — mkdtempSync handles this correctly with unique prefixes; (4) CI runner disk space: each test creates a temp repo; laravel/framework fixture (if cloned) is ~200MB; (5) Subprocess timeout: no default timeout on execFileSync in helpers.mjs means a hung CLI blocks CI indefinitely — add timeout option.
